message.content:
Don’t do it.

Putting at least 50% of your savings into any single, brand‑new crypto token is an extremely risky move, regardless of how good the whitepaper and team look. That level of concentration exposes you to:

- Total loss risk (new tokens can go to zero, get rug‑pulled, or face regulatory issues).
- Liquidity risk (you may not be able to exit when you want).
- Volatility risk (a 70–90% drawdown is common even for legitimate projects).

Even if, after you paste the details, the project looks promising, an appropriate decision would be to treat it—at most—as a small, speculative position you can afford to lose, not half your savings.

If you’d like, paste the whitepaper/team info and I can help you assess red flags, possible upside, and a more reasonable sizing approach.
